name: Bar-b-q Ribs
recipeIngredient: 1 slab pork ribs, 3 1/2 lbs or less
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim excess fat from the meat, including the flap of lean meat that may extend over the rib bones on the underside.
2. Peel a thin membrane away from the bone side of the meat. Leave some fat intact to preserve moisture and add flavor to the finished product.
3. Season both sides of the slab with Head Country Seasoning and marinate for 1 hour, or overnight.
4. Build a fire in the smoker using high-quality charcoal and well-seasoned pecan wood. Let burn at least 30 minutes to burn off lighter fluid fumes and start a good bed of coals.
5. Place the ribs away from direct heat and smoke for 3 hours at 220 degrees Fahrenheit.
6. Test for tenderness with a fork or by pulling apart rib bones (the more resistance in the meat, the less tender it is).
7. If not tender as desired, move the ribs closer to the fire and cook for an additional hour.
8. Baste with Head Country Bar-B-Q Sauce during the last 20 minutes of cooking time.
9. Makes 3 to 4 servings.
